license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.igzcbx6
title: Propagating T5-phages for Fluorescent Staining
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm

steps_raw.instruction: Grow a culture of E.coli ATCC11303 in LB media overnight
The next morning, prewarm LB-agar plates to 37ºC
Pellet 1 mL of the cells in an Eppendorf tube at 10,000 xg, 2 min 00:02:00
Discard the medium
Resuspend the pellet into 1 mL 10 mM MgSO 4 undetermined Magnesium sulfate heptahydrate Contributed by users
Melt 0.6% top agar in the microwave
Prepare sterile 5 mL tubes and pipet 3 mL of melted top agar into them undetermined
Keep tubes in 45ºC heat block or water bath so that the agar stays in a liquid state, but not too hot to kill the cells.
Add 10 µL T5 from the stock (except the control) to 100 µL of resuspended cells
Incubate 5 min at room temperature 00:05:00
Add the infected cells to the molton top agar
Vortex briefly
Pour onto prewarmed LB agar plate and tilt the plate to spread
Let top agar solidify ~30-60 min 01:00:00
Evert plates and return to 37ºC incubator
Let grow ~6-8 hrs 08:00:00
When confluent plaques are seen on the plate, add 5 mL of sterile phage buffer (10 mM MgSO 4 ; 1 mM CaCl 2 ; 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5; 1% gelatin)
Let the plates sit overnight at 4ºC with the buffer on top to gather the phages
The next day, transfer the liquid from the plates to a sterile tube and add 100 µL chloroform and 5 mL phage buffer
Shake for 1 min to let chloroform settle out 00:01:00
Transfer 4 mL of the supernatant to a fresh tube undetermined
Filter the solution with 0.22 µm pore size syringe filter to get rid of all the cellular debris
Store phage at 4ºC. They will stay infective for >1 yr. Fluorescent Staining of T5-phages by Steven W Wilhelm
